data.narrative:
New Limit is a Series B biotech company that has raised $170M+ and carries a last post-money valuation of $810M. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the same sector and stage (Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Neko Health at $1.8B, Asimov at $800M, and Profluent at $600M) plus 3 public biotech comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two methods triangulate to a defensible range. Stage-progression from the $810M base post-money using a typical Series B to next-stage multiplier of 1.9x implies $1.5B. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-sector, same-stage privates yields $1.3B, with a distribution spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public comps anchoring via price-to-sales is not applicable without disclosed revenue. Combining the two applicable methods produces a low estimate of $1.1B, a median of $1.4B, and a high of $1.8B.

This $1.1B–$1.8B range reflects the natural dispersion within early-stage biotech valuations. The current $810M valuation sits below this range, suggesting upside potential at the next financing event. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, competitive positioning within the peer cohort, and stage-specific market conditions at the time of Series C.
